title: ToolCUA: Towards Optimal GUI-Tool Path Orchestration for Computer Use Agents
core_claim: ToolCUA is a staged-training computer-use agent that learns when to stay in GUI actions versus switch to high-level tools, and reports improved OSWorld-MCP performance over comparable baselines.
triage_rationale: This looks like a timely, concrete agent-systems paper with a clear problem setting, a plausible method, and a benchmark result that could support a useful review. It is likely worth reviewing because the GUI-tool orchestration problem is practically relevant and the abstract gives enough technical substance to critique training design and evaluation claims.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark-centric claim may not generalize beyond OSWorld-MCP
Relative improvement is reported without enough detail in the abstract to judge baseline strength
Hybrid-action orchestration results may depend heavily on environment and trajectory synthesis assumptions
